content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10474717/", "title": "A systematic review and meta-analysis of the long-term effects of behavioural physical activity interventions", "summary": "This systematic review and meta-analysis examines the long-term (>=24 months) effects of behavioural physical activity interventions, NOT intermittent fasting. Eight RCTs totaling 8,480 participants were pooled. At 24 months, interventions produced small but statistically significant increases in daily steps (SMD 0.15, 95% CI 0.02-0.28) and moderate-to-vigorous physical activity (SMD 0.18, 95% CI 0.07-0.29). At 36-48 months the effects persisted (steps SMD 0.17, 95% CI 0.07-0.27; MVPA SMD 0.16, 95% CI 0.09-0.23). The paper registers in PROSPERO (CRD42019124377) and applies Cochrane RoB 2. Included studies ran 2010-2018 across USA (N=6), UK (N=3), Australia, Finland, Spain. Confidence assessment: HIGH for the methodology (Cochrane-aligned SR). However, this paper is OFF-TOPIC for the research question about intermittent fasting benefits and risks; it addresses physical activity behavioural interventions, not dietary/fasting regimens. The paper should be excluded or down-weighted for the IF query. Contradictions with IF: none — different domain.
